---
title: "Updating ALSA"
date: 2006-06-10
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by andefeldt on 2006-06-10
So far I'm using the Alsa 1.0.9 drivers supplied by Ubuntu 5.10. However, I'm having huge problems getting SPDIF to work. I thought, that the it might help to update the alsa-drivers from alsa-project.org. But should I uninstall the alsa-packages from Ubuntu or just leave them?

Anders

---

### Post by InsaneBeast on 2006-06-10
What kind of sound card do you have... I had a similar problem with my Sound Blaster Live, and had to install an extra package specific for my hardware.

---

### Post by andefeldt on 2006-06-10
It's an onboard nForce 4 soundcard. It's using the snd_intel8x0 driver (think it's RealTek or something). 

I've been reading so many threads about this problem, but haven't found a solution yet. 

I, however, just looked in my asound.state file. Here I found more of the IEC958 controls (31 to 33) are looking a bit funny in the value section:

```

control.31 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface MIXER
		name 'IEC958 Playback Con Mask'
		value ''
	}
	control.32 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface MIXER
		name 'IEC958 Playback Pro Mask'
		value 
	}
	control.33 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface MIXER
		name 'IEC958 Playback Default'
		value ''
	}
	control.34 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type BOOLEAN
		comment.count 1
		iface MIXER
		name 'IEC958 Playback Switch'
		value true
	}
	control.35 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type INTEGER
		comment.count 1
		comment.range '0 - 3'
		iface MIXER
		name 'IEC958 Playback AC97-SPSA'
		value 0
	}

```

Are yours looking the same way?

Anders

---

### Post by InsaneBeast on 2006-06-10
I don't have the same specific control.## that you have but I do have some similar values for IEC Playback controls:

```
	control.215 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Mask'
		value 
	}
	control.216 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Mask'
		index 1
		value 
	}
	control.217 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Mask'
		index 2
		value 
	}
	control.218 {
		comment.access read
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Mask'
		index 3
		value 
	}
	control.219 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Default'
		value ''
	}
	control.220 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Default'
		index 1
		value ''
	}
	control.221 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Default'
		index 2
		value ''
	}
	control.222 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type IEC958
		comment.count 1
		iface PCM
		device 2
		name 'IEC958 Playback Default'
		index 3
		value ''
	}
	control.223 {
		comment.access 'read write'
		comment.type BOOLEAN
		comment.count 1
		iface MIXER
		name 'SB Live Analog/Digital Output Jack'
		value true
	}
}
```

Have you checked some of the official nvidia forums to see if there are proprietary nvidia linux drivers for your mobo?

---

### Post by andefeldt on 2006-06-10
The official NVidia drivers are OSS only, so I'm not going to use them.

Does your SPDIF work? 

But anyway, how can I update the alsa-drivers with the latest from the source code (from alsa-project.org)?

Anders

---
